political.s: McCormick's FEC data indicates minimal direct political activity with raised donations at $0, while exhibiting a rightward lean with 94.8% Republican contributions ratio (13,650/14,400). Company maintains corporate political engagement through PACs and lobbying.

charity.s: McCormick demonstrates consistent charitable engagement through its 80-year employee giving program and 2024 "135 Days of Giving" campaign which mobilized nearly 7,700 employees to donate nearly $11 million to global charities. Company supports education, health, and community development initiatives.
charity.focus: Education, community development, food security, healthcare

environment.s: McCormick ranks as world's #1 most sustainable company in Food Products sector by Corporate Knights for eight consecutive years (2023). Company achieved A- CDP Climate Change ranking, sustainably sourced 84% of top five branded ingredients by 2022, and reduced Scope 1&2 emissions by 40% since 2020.

labor.s: McCormick employs approximately 13,000 global employees and reports positive recognition as 2021 DiversityInc Top 50 Company. Company emphasizes workplace culture and employee development, though specific OSHA/NLRB violation data was not found in searches.

dei.s: McCormick established explicit 2025 DEI goals targeting 50% women in global leadership and 30% ethnically diverse talent in US leadership positions. Company achieved 44% women representation and 25% EDT as of 2023, with active Employee Ambassador Groups and leadership development programs.

animals.s: Not applicable to spice and seasoning manufacturer

guns.s: Not applicable to spice and seasoning manufacturer

privacy.s: Limited public information available regarding McCormick's data privacy practices and policies

execPay.s: McCormick CEO compensation significantly exceeds worker pay. Current CEO Brendan Foley earns approximately $10.85M annually while average employee salary is $124K, creating approximately 87:1 pay ratio. This exceeds S&P 500 median CEO-to-worker ratio of 324:1.
